description: Add optional tool grouping to the plugin SDK and render grouped tool sections in the side panel. Plugin authors tag tools with a `group` string. The side panel renders tools organized under lightweight section headers with group-level toggle switches. Ungrouped tools fall into an implicit 'Other' section when mixed with grouped tools. Plugins with no groups render flat as today.

userStories.title: Add group field to shared types (ManifestTool and WireToolDef)
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the shared type definitions to include an optional group field so that tool grouping data flows through the entire pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: ManifestTool interface in platform/shared/src/index.ts has `group?: string` field with JSDoc comment
WireToolDef interface in platform/shared/src/index.ts has `group?: string` field with JSDoc comment
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Minimal change — add `/** Tool group for visual grouping in the side panel */ group?: string;` to both interfaces.

- ManifestTool: platform/shared/src/index.ts:91-104. Add after the `icon` field (line 97).
- WireToolDef: platform/shared/src/index.ts:154-166. Add after the `icon` field (line 160).

No logic changes — just type definitions. All existing code continues to work since the field is optional.

userStories.title: Add group field to ToolDefinition in the plugin SDK
userStories.description: As a plugin author, I want to specify an optional group string on my tool definitions so that my tools are visually grouped in the side panel.
acceptanceCriteria: ToolDefinition interface in platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts has `group?: string` field with JSDoc comment
JSDoc explains that the group is used for visual grouping in the side panel and is optional
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: Add `/** Optional group name for visual grouping in the side panel. Tools with the same group are rendered together under a section header. */ group?: string;` to the ToolDefinition interface in platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts:53-71. Add it after the `icon` field. The defineTool factory (line 73-76) is a pass-through identity function — no changes needed there.

userStories.title: Pass group through the build pipeline (plugin-tools)
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the plugin build tool to include the group field in the generated tools.json manifest so that group data reaches the MCP server.
acceptanceCriteria: generateToolsManifest in platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts includes `group: tool.group` in the ManifestTool output (only when the field is present)
A plugin tool with group='Messages' produces a tools.json entry containing `"group": "Messages"`
A plugin tool without a group produces a tools.json entry with no group field
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
In platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts, the `generateToolsManifest` function (line 497-508) maps each tool to a ManifestTool. After line 504 (`icon: tool.icon || 'wrench'`), add: `...(tool.group ? { group: tool.group } : {})`. Use spread-conditional so the field is omitted entirely when undefined, keeping the manifest clean for plugins that don't use grouping.

The existing tests in platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.test.ts may need a test case added to verify group passthrough.

userStories.title: Pass group through the MCP server wire serialization and file watcher
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the MCP server to forward the group field from the manifest to the extension via the wire protocol so that the side panel can render groups.
acceptanceCriteria: serializePluginForExtension in platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts includes `group` in the tool wire payload when present in the manifest
parseManifest in platform/mcp-server/src/file-watcher.ts reads the `group` field from tools.json and includes it in the parsed ManifestTool
A tool with group='Messages' in tools.json results in group='Messages' in the WireToolDef sent to the extension
A tool without a group results in no group field in the WireToolDef
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Two files to update:

1. platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts — In `serializePluginForExtension` (line 109), the tools mapping (lines 137-145) constructs each wire tool object. After `icon: t.icon` (line 141), add: `...(t.group ? { group: t.group } : {})`. This conditionally includes the group only when present.

2. platform/mcp-server/src/file-watcher.ts — In `parseManifest` (around line 254-276), where each ManifestTool is constructed from the raw JSON. After `icon` is parsed (line 260), add: `const group = typeof obj.group === 'string' ? obj.group : undefined;`. Then in the `tools.push({...})` call (line 269-276), add `...(group ? { group } : {})` to the object.

3. platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts — In the WireToolDef mapping (lines 193-206), add group extraction: `...(typeof obj.group === 'string' ? { group: obj.group } : {})` in the returned object.

userStories.title: Render grouped tool sections in the side panel PluginCard
userStories.description: As a user, I want tools in an expanded plugin card to be visually organized under group section headers so that I can quickly scan and find tools in plugins with many tools.
acceptanceCriteria: When a plugin has tools with group values, tools are rendered organized under section headers showing the group name
Group headers use font-head at text-xs size with uppercase tracking and muted-foreground color, creating a clear visual hierarchy below the plugin name
Each group header has a convenience Switch that toggles all tools in that group
Tools without a group value are rendered under an 'Other' section at the bottom when mixed with grouped tools
When no tools in a plugin have groups, the tool list renders flat with no section headers (same as current behavior)
Group order follows first appearance in the tools array (manifest order)
Tool order within each group follows manifest order
The tool filter (search) still works correctly — filtered tools show under their group headers, empty groups are hidden
The filter count ('N of M tools') still displays correctly
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
This is the main UI story. Changes are in platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx. **Grouping logic:** Before rendering, group `visibleTools` by their `group` field. Preserve manifest order: iterate tools once, collect groups in first-seen order, bucket tools into each group. Tools with no `group` go into a special bucket. If all tools lack groups, render the flat list as-is (no headers). If any tool has a group, render all groups including an 'Other' group for ungrouped tools at the bottom. **Group header design:** The group header is a simple div row — NOT a new retro primitive. It should feel like a lightweight label within the card content area, not a nested card or accordion. Design: ```
{groupName}
``` Key visual decisions: - `font-head` (Archivo Black) at `text-xs` with `uppercase tracking-wider` — creates a distinct label feel that sits between the plugin name hierarchy and tool names - `bg-muted/20` — subtle background tint to differentiate the header row from tool rows - `py-1` — thinner than tool rows (py-1.5) so headers feel like labels, not content - `border-b border-border` — consistent with tool row borders - Group-level Switch uses the existing retro Switch component — toggles all tools in the group **Group toggle handler:** When the group Switch is toggled, it should call handleToggleTool for each tool in the group. Or better — create a handleToggleGroup function similar to the existing handleToggleAll, but scoped to tools in a specific group. It should use the same optimistic update + rollback pattern as handleToggleAll (lines 59-71). The group toggle needs a server-side batch toggle. For now, call setToolEnabled for each tool in the group sequentially, or better — reuse setAllToolsEnabled with a filter. Check if the existing bridge functions support this. If not, iterate tools in the group and call setToolEnabled for each, with a single optimistic UI update. **Alternating row backgrounds:** The existing `even:bg-muted/10` on ToolRow uses CSS :even pseudo-class which counts all sibling elements. With group headers interspersed, the even/odd pattern will shift. This is acceptable — the alternating pattern still provides visual rhythm within each group. Alternatively, reset the counter per group by wrapping each group's tools in a div. **Existing retro components used:** Switch (for group toggle), no new primitives needed. **Files to modify:** - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx — main changes here - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts — may need a new bridge function for group-level toggle, or reuse existing ones

userStories.title: Add tool groups to all existing plugins
userStories.description: As a user, I want the existing plugins (Slack, Discord, GitHub, Notion) to have their tools organized into meaningful groups so that the grouping feature is immediately useful.
acceptanceCriteria: Slack plugin tools have groups: Messages, Channels, Users, Reactions, Files, DMs
Discord plugin tools have groups: Messages, Channels, Users, Reactions, DMs, Servers
GitHub plugin tools have groups: Issues, Pull Requests, Comments, Repositories, Users, Reactions
Notion plugin tools have groups: Pages, Databases, Users, Blocks
Each plugin builds successfully with `cd plugins/<name> && npm run build`
tools.json for each plugin contains the group field on every tool
userStories.notes:
Add `group` to every `defineTool()` call in each plugin's tool files. The group names should map to domain entities.

Plugin tool files are typically at `plugins/<name>/src/tools/*.ts` or `plugins/<name>/src/tools.ts`. Read the existing tool definitions to understand the tool names, then assign groups based on the domain entity each tool operates on.

**Slack** (plugins/slack/src/tools/):
- Messages: send_message, read_messages, read_thread, search_messages, edit_message, delete_message
- Channels: list_channels, create_channel, get_channel_info, set_channel_topic, set_channel_purpose
- Users: list_members, list_users, get_user_profile, invite_to_channel
- Reactions: pin_message, unpin_message, add_reaction, remove_reaction
- Files: upload_file, list_files
- DMs: open_dm

**Discord** (plugins/discord/src/tools/):
- Messages: send_message, read_messages, read_thread, search_messages, edit_message, delete_message
- Channels: list_channels, create_channel, get_channel_info, create_thread
- Users: list_members, get_user_profile
- Reactions: pin_message, unpin_message, add_reaction, remove_reaction
- DMs: list_dms, open_dm
- Servers: list_guilds

**GitHub** (plugins/github/src/tools/):
- Issues: create_issue, get_issue, update_issue, search_issues, list_issues
- Pull Requests: create_pull_request, get_pull_request, merge_pull_request, list_pull_requests
- Comments: create_comment, list_comments
- Repositories: list_repositories, get_repository, create_repository, list_branches, get_file_content, create_or_update_file
- Users: get_user_profile, list_organization_members, list_notifications
- Reactions: add_reaction

**Notion** (plugins/notion/src/tools/):
- Pages: create_page, get_page, update_page, delete_page, list_pages, search
- Databases: get_database, query_database, create_database_item
- Users: get_user, list_users
- Blocks: get_block_children, append_block

Note: Notion's 'search' tool is a general search that returns pages — put it in Pages group.

Build each plugin after making changes: `cd plugins/<name> && npm run build`. Each plugin is standalone (not in npm workspaces), so they build independently.

IMPORTANT: The plugin SDK version these plugins depend on must have the `group` field (from US-002). The plugins import from `@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk` which is the local workspace version. After US-002 is committed, the SDK types will be available. If TypeScript errors occur because the SDK hasn't been rebuilt, run `npm run build` from the repo root first to rebuild all platform packages.
